title: composit-report
description: Inventory of infrastructure resources detected in a workspace. Generated by `composit scan`. Represents the IS-state that `composit diff` compares against a Compositfile's SHOULD-state.

^x-.description: Extension field. Implementations MAY attach vendor- or domain-specific data under any top-level key prefixed with `x-`. Consumers unaware of a given extension MUST ignore it.

workspace.description: Workspace identifier. Typically the target directory name or an explicit workspace label.

generated.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the scan was run.

scanner_version.description: Semantic version of the composit CLI that produced this report.

scan_mode.description: Whether the scan contacted provider manifests. 'offline' means --no-providers was set (or no providers were configured), which consumers like `composit diff` use to downgrade warnings that only apply when providers were actually checked. Optional for backward compatibility with reports produced before this field was introduced; a missing value should be treated as 'online'.

providers.description: Providers discovered through MCP configs, manifest URLs, or explicit CLI flags.

resources.description: All detected resources. Concrete fields beyond the required ones depend on resource_type (e.g. docker_service has image/ports; workflow has runs_on/jobs).

endpoint.description: Base URL of the provider. Manifest is expected at <endpoint>/.well-known/composit.json.

protocol.description: Transport protocol for the provider. 'mcp' is canonical for MCP-native providers.

capabilities.description: Capability types advertised by the provider (e.g. scheduling, events, knowledge).

status.description: Reachability at scan time. 'unknown' when no live check was performed.

auth_mode.description: How much of the provider was observed during the scan (RFC 002). 'public' = only /.well-known/composit.json. 'contract' = authenticated contract URL was also fetched. 'unreachable' = public manifest could not be loaded. Optional for backward compatibility with reports produced before this field existed; consumers SHOULD treat a missing value as 'public'.

auth_error.description: Diagnostic when `auth_mode == 'public'` but governance declared trust = 'contract'. Known values: 'auth_missing', 'auth_type_not_advertised', 'unauthorized', 'fetch_failed', 'invalid_contract_body'. See RFC 002 §scan/diff behaviour and RFC 003 for semantics.

ContractInfo.description: Bookkeeping subset of the RFC 003 contract-manifest response, recorded when `auth_mode == 'contract'`. Present only when the scanner successfully parsed the contract body. `composit diff` emits `contract_expired` when `expires_at` is in the past.

id.description: Stable identifier for the (contract, identity) pair. Opaque to consumers.

issued_at.description: When the contract relationship started. ISO 8601.

expires_at.description: When the contract becomes invalid. ISO 8601. Past timestamp triggers `contract_expired` (Error) in `composit diff`.

pricing_tier.description: Short label identifying the identity's tier (provider-defined, e.g. 'free', 'team', 'enterprise'). Informational.

Resource.description: A single detected resource. The `type` field determines which additional fields are meaningful. Unknown fields are preserved to keep the format forward-compatible.

type.description: Canonical resource type. Scanners emit stable type strings (e.g. docker_service, terraform_resource, cron_job). See docs/schemas/resource-types.md for the registry.

name.description: Human-readable identifier within its type. Optional for types where a path is sufficient (e.g. env_file).

path.description: Repo-relative path. Always prefixed with ./ when present.

provider.description: Name of the provider this resource belongs to, if any.

created.description: ISO-8601 date of first git creation, when attribution is available.

created_by.description: Attribution of the creating author. Prefix indicates kind: 'human:<name>', 'agent:<id>' (claude, copilot, ...).

detected_by.description: ID of the scanner that detected this resource (e.g. docker, terraform, mcp_config).

estimated_cost.description: Best-effort cost estimate as '<amount> <currency>/<period>' (e.g. '12 EUR/month'). Aggregated into summary.estimated_monthly_cost.

agent_assisted.description: Resources where a human authored the commit but an agent appears as Co-Authored-By.

human_created.description: Resources authored purely by humans (no agent assistance detected).

auto_detected.description: Resources without attribution (e.g. detected from filesystem, no git history).

estimated_monthly_cost.description: Aggregated monthly cost in a single currency (currently EUR).
